info.title: CPHS Go1 API
info.description:
Control a real **Unitree Go1 quadruped robot** over HTTP. This API drives the
physical dog in the lab — what you send here happens in the real world.

🎮 **Interactive playground with live video & audio: [https://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/playground](https://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/playground)**

## Safety rules (read first)

- **Dead man's switch**: the robot auto-stops 5 seconds after the last `/move`
  command. To keep walking, re-send `/move` at least every 5 s.
- **Emergency stop**: `POST /api/c2/stop` — always works while connected.
- **One driver at a time**: connecting takes over the robot; a running
  dashboard example is stopped automatically (shared UDP port).
- Velocity, pose, and action limits are enforced server-side, but the robot
  still needs ~1 m of clear space around it. Look at the video feed before
  you move it.

## Authentication

Every endpoint — HTTP and WebSocket alike — requires **HTTP Basic Auth**.
Staff will give you a username and password out-of-band (not published here).
Export them once and every example below picks them up automatically:

```bash
export CPHS_USER="<given by staff>"
export CPHS_PASS="<given by staff>"
```

`curl` takes credentials via `-u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS"`. WebSocket tools
that don't support Basic Auth directly (like `websocat`) need a
pre-encoded `Authorization` header instead:

```bash
CPHS_AUTH_B64=$(printf '%s:%s' "$CPHS_USER" "$CPHS_PASS" | base64)
```

A missing or wrong password gets a `401` on HTTP endpoints, or an immediate
close on WebSocket endpoints.

## Quickstart (60 seconds)

```bash
BASE=https://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de
export CPHS_USER="<given by staff>"
export CPHS_PASS="<given by staff>"

curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/connect            # take control (robot stands)
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/action -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
     -d '{"action": "dance1"}'               # 🕺 (~16 s)
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" $BASE/api/c2/status                     # watch state: performing_action → idle
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/disconnect         # release control
```

## Example workflows

### 1. Wake up and dance

```bash
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/connect
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/action -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"action": "stand"}'
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/action -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"action": "dance1"}'
sleep 16
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/disconnect
```

### 2. Drive a square (keepalive pattern)

Each `/move` lasts at most 5 s (dead man's switch), so a leg of the square is
one `/move` + `sleep` ≤ 5 s. Forward at 0.3 m/s for 3 s ≈ 0.9 m, then turn 90°
(1.57 rad at 0.8 rad/s ≈ 2 s):

```bash
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/connect
for i in 1 2 3 4; do
  curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/move -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
       -d '{"vx": 0.3, "vy": 0, "yaw_speed": 0}'
  sleep 3
  curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/move -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
       -d '{"vx": 0, "vy": 0, "yaw_speed": 0.8}'
  sleep 2
done
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/stop
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/disconnect
```

### 3. Read sensors while walking

```bash
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/connect
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/move -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
     -d '{"vx": 0.2, "vy": 0, "yaw_speed": 0}'
for i in $(seq 5); do curl -s -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" $BASE/api/c2/state | python3 -m json.tool; sleep 1; done
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/c2/stop
```

Watch `imu_gyroscope`, `foot_force` (per-leg ground contact), and `velocity`.

### 4. See through the robot's eyes

```bash
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -o shot.jpg $BASE/api/system/camera/snapshot   # single frame
# open this URL in a browser tab: $BASE/api/system/video.mjpeg   # live stream (browser will prompt for credentials)
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" $BASE/api/system/cameras                       # list all 5 cameras
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X POST $BASE/api/system/camera -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
     -d '{"camera_id": "chin"}'                     # switch camera
```

### 5. WebSockets from the shell / Python

```bash
# live logs
websocat --basic-auth "$CPHS_AUTH_B64" wss://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/api/system/logs/ws
# robot microphone → your speakers (Linux)
curl -u "$CPHS_USER:$CPHS_PASS" -X PATCH $BASE/api/system/audio -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"mode": "listen"}'
websocat -b --basic-auth "$CPHS_AUTH_B64" wss://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/api/system/audio | aplay -f S16_LE -r 48000 -c 1
```

```python
# save one video frame (pip install websockets)
import asyncio, base64, os, websockets

auth = base64.b64encode(f"{os.environ['CPHS_USER']}:{os.environ['CPHS_PASS']}".encode()).decode()

async def main():
    async with websockets.connect(
            "wss://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/api/system/video",
            additional_headers={"Authorization": f"Basic {auth}"}) as ws:
        while True:
            msg = await ws.recv()
            if isinstance(msg, bytes):          # binary = JPEG frame
                open("frame.jpg", "wb").write(msg)
                break                            # text = JSON status, keep waiting

asyncio.run(main())
```

## WebSocket protocol reference

Swagger can't display WebSockets, so here they are:

| URL | Direction | Messages |
|---|---|---|
| `wss://…/api/system/video` | server → you | binary = JPEG frame; text = `{"status": "connected"\|"no_signal"}` |
| `wss://…/api/system/audio` | bidirectional | binary = PCM **48 kHz, S16_LE, mono** (both directions); send text `{"mode": "off"\|"listen"\|"talk"}` to switch |
| `wss://…/api/system/logs/ws` | server → you | text = JSON log lines |
| `wss://…/api/system/system/ws` | server → you | text = JSON system status every 2 s |

Audio modes: `listen` = robot mic streams to you; `talk` = your PCM plays on
the robot's speaker; `off` = silence. Also settable via `PATCH /api/system/audio`.

## Errors you will meet

| Status | Meaning | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 409 | Controller not connected | `POST /api/c2/connect` first |
| 503 on `/connect` | Robot unreachable or SDK missing | Is the robot powered on? Ask staff. |
| 503 on camera endpoints | No frames yet | Wait a few seconds after power-on; try `/api/system/stream/status` |
| 400 on `/action` | Unknown action name | See the action list in `POST /api/c2/action` |

post.summary: Start controller and connect to robot
post.description: Start the persistent 500Hz UDP control loop to the robot.

200.description: Controller started, robot in forced stand.

503.description: SDK not available or already connected.

post.summary: Stop controller and release UDP port
post.description: Stop the 500Hz control loop and release the UDP port.

Set continuous velocity on the robot (walk, strafe, turn).

The robot keeps moving until /stop, /disconnect, or the dead man's switch
fires (5 s without a new /move). See the request schema below for velocity
ranges and gait types.

200.description: Velocity applied. Robot is now moving.

post.description: Stop all robot movement and cancel any in-progress action.

post.summary: Execute a robot action (dance, jump, wiggle, etc.)
post.description:
Execute a named action on the robot (dance, jump, sit, stand, ...).

See the request schema below for the full action catalog, timing notes,
and which actions need a preparation pause.
Set the robot's body orientation and height while standing.

get.description: Get the latest sensor data from the Go1's HighState.

200.description: Latest sensor data from the robot.

503.description: No state received from robot yet.

get.description: Get the current state of the C2 controller.

Overall system health: robot reachability, current robot IP, uptime.

Good first call to check whether the robot is on and reachable before
POST /api/c2/connect.

Return the most recent server log lines (newest last).

`limit` caps the number of lines (default 200). For a live feed, use the
WebSocket at /api/system/logs/ws (see the intro above).

Audit trail of every command sent to the robot, by anyone, newest last.

Useful to see what the robot was told to do — including by other students
and the dashboard. `limit` caps the number of entries (default 50).
Current speaker volume, mute state, and intercom mode.

patch.description:
Update speaker volume, mute, and/or intercom mode in one call.

Send only the fields you want to change, e.g. `{"volume": 80}` or
`{"mode": "listen"}`. Mode 'listen' streams the robot mic to the audio
WebSocket; 'talk' plays your WebSocket PCM on the robot's speaker.

get.description:
Run audio hardware diagnostics on the robot's head computer (~10 s).

Returns raw command output — useful when audio is silent to check whether
the speaker device is busy or the volume is at 0.

schema.title: Response Audio Diagnostics Api System Audio Diag Get

get.description: List the five cameras (front, chin, left, right, belly) and which one is active.

schema.title: Response List Cameras Api System Cameras Get

post.description:
Switch the active camera feeding the video stream and snapshots.

Takes a few seconds to reconnect; check GET /api/system/stream/status.

get.description: Health of the streaming pipeline: is video flowing, is audio available.

Return the most recent frame from the active camera as a JPEG image.

Click **Try it out** to see through the robot's eyes right here in the docs.
From a shell: `curl -o shot.jpg https://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/api/system/camera/snapshot`

Switch cameras first with `POST /api/system/camera`.

200.description: Latest JPEG frame from the active camera.

503.description: No frame available yet (camera starting up, robot off, or stream down).

get.description: Live video as a Motion-JPEG stream (`multipart/x-mixed-replace`). The simplest way to watch the robot live: open `https://go1.cphs.mylab.th-luebeck.de/api/system/video.mjpeg` in a browser tab, or embed it: ``. Streams until you disconnect. For frame-by-frame access with lower latency, use the WebSocket at `/api/system/video` (see the intro above).

200.description: Endless MJPEG stream. Open in a browser tab or an tag — no JavaScript needed.

503.description: No frames available yet (camera starting up or robot off).

ActionRequest.title: ActionRequest
ActionRequest.description:
Execute a discrete robot action.

Simple actions (immediate):
- 'stand': forced stand with active stabilization (mode 1).
- 'sit': idle/damping, robot relaxes and sits down (mode 0).
- 'stand_up': position-controlled rise to standing (mode 6).
- 'stand_down': position-controlled crouch down (mode 5).
- 'damping': all joints go compliant / soft e-stop (mode 7).
- 'recovery_stand': get up from a fallen position (mode 8, must be in damping first).

Special actions (require ~1s preparation in force stand, then execute):
- 'jump_yaw': jump with yaw rotation (left direction).
- 'straight_hand': "beg/pray" pose — front legs extend forward, balances on hind legs (~8s).
- 'dance1': pre-choreographed dance routine 1 (~16s).
- 'dance2': pre-choreographed dance routine 2 (~16s).
- 'backflip': backflip (may be EDU-only / firmware-gated).

Synthesized actions:
- 'wiggle': butt wiggle — rapidly oscillates body roll in force stand. Runs until /stop.

state.title: State
state.description: Controller state: 'idle' for simple actions, 'performing_action' for special/wiggle.

ActionResponse.title: ActionResponse
ActionResponse.description:
Confirms the action was started.

For special actions, the robot first holds force stand for ~1 second,
then performs the action. The controller state is 'performing_action'
during execution and returns to 'idle' when complete.

AudioConfigRequest.title: AudioConfigRequest
AudioConfigRequest.description: Update audio settings. All fields optional — only provided fields are applied.

muted.title: Muted
muted.description: True if the speaker is muted.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Intercom mode: 'off', 'listen' (robot mic → you), or 'talk' (your audio → robot speaker).

AudioState.title: AudioState
AudioState.description: Current state of the robot's speaker and intercom.

jetson_ip.title: Jetson Ip
jetson_ip.description: Onboard computer this camera is attached to (internal).

active.title: Active
active.description: True if this camera currently feeds the video stream.

CameraInfo.title: CameraInfo
CameraInfo.description: One of the Go1's five cameras.

robot_ip.title: Robot Ip
robot_ip.description: Robot IP address. Defaults to current dashboard robot IP (typically 192.168.123.161).

local_port.title: Local Port
local_port.description: Local UDP port for the control loop. Must not conflict with other processes.

setup_streams.title: Setup Streams
setup_streams.description: Auto-run Jetson setup for video/audio streams after connecting.

ConnectRequest.title: ConnectRequest
ConnectRequest.description:
Start the persistent 500Hz control loop to the robot.

If robot_ip is omitted, the current robot IP from the dashboard is used
(default: 192.168.123.161). Any running dashboard example is automatically
stopped to free the shared UDP port.

robot_ip.title: Robot Ip
robot_ip.description: IP address of the connected robot. Null when disconnected.

local_port.title: Local Port
local_port.description: Local UDP port in use. Null when disconnected.

current_velocity.description: Current velocity being sent. Null when disconnected.

current_mode.title: Current Mode
current_mode.description: SDK mode being sent: 0=idle, 1=force stand, 2=walk, 5=stand down, 6=stand up, 7=damping, 8=recovery, 9=backflip, 10=jump yaw, 11=straight hand, 12=dance1, 13=dance2.

current_action.title: Current Action
current_action.description: Name of the action being performed, or null if none.

error.title: Error
error.description: Last error message, or null if no error.

ControllerStatus.title: ControllerStatus
ControllerStatus.description:
Current state of the C2 controller.

The controller has four states:
- 'disconnected': no UDP loop running, port is free.
- 'idle': loop running, robot in force stand, no velocity.
- 'moving': loop running, velocity commands being sent.
- 'performing_action': a special action (dance, jump, wiggle, etc.) is executing.

vx.title: Vx
vx.description: Forward (+) / backward (-) velocity. Range: [-1.0, 1.0]. Use up to 3.5 with gait_type=2 (run).

yaw_speed.title: Yaw Speed
yaw_speed.description: Counter-clockwise (+) / clockwise (-) yaw rotation in rad/s. Range: [-4.0, 4.0].

gait_type.title: Gait Type
gait_type.description: Gait pattern: 0=idle, 1=trot (default, vx up to 1.5 m/s), 2=trot running (fast, vx up to 3.5 m/s), 3=climb stair (slow, vx up to 0.25 m/s), 4=trot obstacle.

foot_raise_height.title: Foot Raise Height
foot_raise_height.description: Foot lift height in meters. Higher values for rough terrain. Default: 0.08.

MoveRequest.title: MoveRequest
MoveRequest.description:
Set continuous velocity on the robot.

The robot walks at the given velocity until a /stop, /disconnect, or the
dead man's switch triggers (5 seconds with no new /move command).

Velocity reference:
- vx=0.3, vy=0, yaw=0: walk forward
- vx=-0.2, vy=0, yaw=0: walk backward
- vx=0, vy=0.2, yaw=0: strafe left
- vx=0, vy=0, yaw=1.0: rotate left in place
- vx=0, vy=0, yaw=-1.0: rotate right in place
- vx=0.3, vy=0, yaw=0.5: walk forward while turning left

To keep the robot moving indefinitely, resend /move periodically
(at least every 5 seconds) to reset the dead man's switch.

roll.title: Roll
roll.description: Body roll (lean left/right) in radians. Range: [-0.75, 0.75].

pitch.title: Pitch
pitch.description: Body pitch (look up/down) in radians. Negative = look up. Range: [-0.75, 0.75].

yaw.title: Yaw
yaw.description: Body yaw (twist left/right) in radians. Range: [-0.6, 0.6].

body_height.title: Body Height
body_height.description: Body height delta from default (0.31m). Negative = lower. Range: [-0.13, 0.03]. E.g. -0.1 crouches, 0.03 stretches up.

PoseRequest.title: PoseRequest
PoseRequest.description:
Set body pose while in force stand (mode 1).

Controls the robot's body orientation and height without moving.
Useful for looking around, leaning, or adjusting posture.

All angles are in radians. Height is a delta from the default
standing height (0.31m).

pose.description: The pose that was set (clamped to valid ranges).

imu_quaternion.title: Imu Quaternion
imu_quaternion.description: IMU orientation as quaternion [w, x, y, z].

imu_gyroscope.title: Imu Gyroscope
imu_gyroscope.description: IMU angular velocity [x, y, z] in rad/s.

imu_accelerometer.title: Imu Accelerometer
imu_accelerometer.description: IMU linear acceleration [x, y, z] in m/s^2. Includes gravity (~[0, 0, -9.81] when level).

velocity.title: Velocity
velocity.description: Estimated body velocity [vx, vy, vz] in m/s (robot frame).

yaw_speed.title: Yaw Speed
yaw_speed.description: Estimated yaw rotation speed in rad/s.

foot_force.title: Foot Force
foot_force.description: Foot contact force for each leg [FR, FL, RR, RL]. Non-zero when foot touches ground.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Current robot mode: 0=idle, 1=force stand, 2=walking, 5=stand down, 6=stand up, 7=damping, 8=recovery, 9=backflip, 10=jump yaw, 11=straight hand, 12=dance1, 13=dance2.

battery_voltage.title: Battery Voltage
battery_voltage.description: Battery voltage in volts (if available from HighState).

battery_current.title: Battery Current
battery_current.description: Battery current draw in amps (if available from HighState).

RobotState.title: RobotState
RobotState.description:
Live sensor data from the Go1's HighState, updated at 500Hz.

Data is read from the robot's Raspberry Pi (192.168.123.161) via
the high-level UDP protocol. All values come from the onboard IMU,
motor encoders, and foot force sensors.

StopResponse.title: StopResponse
StopResponse.description: Confirms robot stopped and entered forced stand.

video_connected.title: Video Connected
video_connected.description: True if frames are currently arriving from the robot.

audio_available.title: Audio Available
audio_available.description: True if the robot's audio hardware is reachable.

camera_url.title: Camera Url
camera_url.description: Internal source URL of the active camera stream.

tunnel_mode.title: Tunnel Mode
tunnel_mode.description: True when the robot is reached via the remote tunnel.

StreamStatus.title: StreamStatus
StreamStatus.description: Health of the video/audio streaming pipeline.

SwitchCameraRequest.title: SwitchCameraRequest
SwitchCameraRequest.description: Select which camera feeds the video stream and snapshots.

VelocityInfo.title: VelocityInfo
VelocityInfo.description: Current velocity being sent to the robot.

tags.name: Control
tags.description: Make the robot do things. Lifecycle: `connect` → `move`/`action`/`pose` → `stop` → `disconnect`. Commands go to the robot over a persistent 500 Hz UDP loop.

tags.name: Sensors
tags.description: Live robot state: IMU, estimated velocity, per-leg foot forces, battery, and the controller state machine.

tags.name: Camera
tags.description: Five cameras (front, chin, left, right, belly). Plain-HTTP snapshot and MJPEG endpoints below; low-latency frames via the WebSocket `/api/system/video` (see intro).

tags.name: Audio
tags.description: Speaker volume/mute and intercom mode. PCM streaming runs over the WebSocket `/api/system/audio` (see intro).

tags.name: Logs & History
tags.description: Server logs and the audit trail of every command anyone sent to the robot.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Robot reachability and overall stream health.
